# Representing composed distributions

## Why?

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
- As we have seen, many epidemiological delays can be thought of as
  **composed** of delays between events
- The serial interval is a generation time plus an incubation
  difference, observed through primary and secondary censoring and
  truncation
- The same few biases recur, in different combinations
- We want to write a delay down once, from reusable, tested parts, and
  reuse it across models
:::
::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-note}
A longer take on this idea of building models from parts:
[Juniper seminar (YouTube)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FQYOqGnbJWA)
:::
:::
::::

## CensoredDistributions.jl

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="44%"}
- A Julia package, in effect a version of `primarycensored`
- Censoring and truncation are just **wrappers** around a delay
- The result is a distribution you can `pdf`, `rand`, and **fit with
  Turing.jl**
:::
::: {.column width="56%"}
```julia
using CensoredDistributions, Distributions, Turing

# a censored, truncated delay
d = double_interval_censored(
    Gamma(2, 3); upper = 15, interval = 1)

# fit it with Turing.jl
@model function fit(y, w)
    α ~ truncated(Normal(2, 1), 0, Inf)
    θ ~ truncated(Normal(3, 1), 0, Inf)
    y ~ weight(double_interval_censored(
        Gamma(α, θ); upper = 15, interval = 1), w)
end
```

::: {.attribution}
[CensoredDistributions.jl](https://github.com/EpiAware/CensoredDistributions.jl)
:::
:::
::::

## Building distributions from parts

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="42%"}
- In development: compose the delays between events
- Additive chains, competing outcomes by racing hazards, or a data
  field that selects the sub-model
- Then wrap the whole delay in the observation model
- Covariates can modify it — on the **hazard scale**, or within
  individual delay **leaves**
:::
::: {.column width="58%"}
```julia
# leaves: censored, truncated delays
inc = double_interval_censored(Gamma(2, 1); interval = 1)

# an additive chain of delays
si = Sequential(generation_time, inc)

# competing outcomes, by racing hazards
resolve = competing(:death => death, :discharge => discharge)

# a record field selects the sub-model
delay = selecting(:index => inc, :sourced => si)

# assemble the whole record
tree = compose((delay = delay, resolution = resolve))
```

::: {.attribution}
In-flight composer work:
[CensoredDistributions.jl#363](https://github.com/EpiAware/CensoredDistributions.jl/pull/363)
:::
:::
::::

## Backing different model types

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="42%"}
- Composed delays back different model types
- A delay used directly for individual or count models; dwell-time
  delays lowered onto a reaction network for a mechanistic one
:::
::: {.column width="58%"}
```julia
# individual level: likelihood / simulate
logpdf(d, t);   rand(d)

# counts: convolve with incidence (EpiNow2)
expected = convolve(infections, d)

# mechanistic: lower dwell-time delays onto a
# Catalyst reaction network (linear-chain trick)
using Catalyst
seir = seir_reaction_network(latent, infectious)
```

::: {.callout-note}
Simulation and `logpdf` for individual data, convolution for counts, or
— via the **linear-chain trick** — a Catalyst reaction network for a
compartmental model.
:::
:::
::::
